m1.q_en: What are Flutter’s three architecture layers from top to bottom?
m1.explain_en: Framework (Dart: widgets, layout…) → Engine (C++: Skia, text…) → Embedder (ties to the OS).

m1.q_en: How does Flutter mainly draw the UI compared to React Native?
options_en: Uses native XML/Storyboard views for every screen
Flutter engine + Skia paints pixels; Flutter UI does not bridge to native views
m1.explain_en: Flutter renders via its engine/Skia; RN bridges to native views.

m1.q_en: When should you add native code (Kotlin/Swift) in a Flutter project?
options_en: When you need deep platform features (camera, payment SDKs…) via MethodChannel/EventChannel
m1.explain_en: Platform-specific OS/SDK bits integrate via platform channels; Flutter still paints the Flutter UI.

m1.q_en: Where is the usual entry point of a Flutter/Dart app?
JIT vs AOT in Dart/Flutter (quick review) — which is more accurate?
options_en: JIT in dev; AOT on mobile release for near-native performance
m1.explain_en: Dev: JIT + hot reload; mobile release: AOT.

m1.q_en: Short pipeline from widget to screen?
options_en: Widget tree → Element + RenderObject (layout/paint) → compositing → GPU
m1.explain_en: The framework diffs widgets, layout/paint via render objects, then compositing.

m1.q_en: When is a Platform Channel (`MethodChannel`) used?
options_en: Call native (Kotlin/Swift) from Dart for OS/SDK-specific features
m1.explain_en: Bridges Dart ↔ native for camera, sensors, third-party SDKs, etc.

options_en: `const` requires compile-time values; `final` is single assignment but can be runtime (e.g. `DateTime.now()`)
m2.explain_en: `const` is compile-time constant; `final` is assigned once at runtime.

options_en: `?.` safely accesses if non-null; `??` supplies a value when the left side is null
m2.explain_en: `?.` short-circuits; `??` picks the right side when the left is null.

m2.q_en: Why do `async`/`await` help keep the Flutter UI responsive during network calls?
options_en: Because network runs on the UI thread
Heavy work is scheduled for later without blocking the Dart isolate synchronously for too long
Because Flutter has no event loop
m2.explain_en: `await` yields; I/O should not block the isolate inside long synchronous callbacks.

m2.q_en: How does a broadcast stream differ from a single-subscription stream?
options_en: Broadcast allows many listeners; single-subscription is usually one sequential listener
m2.explain_en: Single-subscription: one main listener; broadcast: many (e.g. `StreamController.broadcast()`).

m2.q_en: In Dart, which operator is **integer division** for two `int` values?
m2.explain_en: `/` with ints may yield double; `~/` is truncating integer division.

m2.q_en: `map` on `Iterable` returns a lazy iterable — which is true?
options_en: It is already a full `List` right after `map`
You usually need `toList()` / `for-in` to actually run and collect results

m2.q_en: `try { } on SpecificException catch (e) { }` — what is `on` for?
options_en: Catches a specific type before falling through to a general `catch`

m2.q_en: How do you compare two values of the same `enum` in Dart?
m2.explain_en: Enum values are constant objects — `==` works for the same enum type.

options_en: `implements` requires you to reimplement the public API with no inherited implementation; `extends` inherits code
m3.explain_en: Every Dart class is an implicit interface; `implements` is the contract—you write the bodies.

m3.q_en: After synchronous code, microtask vs event queue order (exam model)?
options_en: Drain all microtasks before taking the next event from the event queue
m3.explain_en: Microtasks run with higher priority than the next event after sync code finishes.

m3.q_en: What are factory constructors commonly used for?
options_en: JSON parsing, singletons, returning subtypes or cached instances—not always a new instance per call
m3.explain_en: Factories flex object creation (`fromJson`, cache, etc.).

m3.q_en: What does `with MyMixin` on a class do?
options_en: Mixes mixin implementation into the class (subject to `on` constraints)
m3.explain_en: Mixins reuse behavior; `on` limits which classes can use `with`.

m3.q_en: In a List literal, `[a]` vs `[...a]` when `a` is a `List` — difference?
options_en: `[a]` nests a List as one element; `[...a]` spreads elements of `a`
`microtask` uses the microtask queue; `Future((){})` usually schedules on the event queue
m3.explain_en: Microtasks are drained before the next event after sync work.

m3.q_en: Repository pattern (idea): what should the UI **not** do directly?
m3.explain_en: Layering: repository owns endpoints + parsing + errors; easier to mock in tests.

m4.q_en: After `setState`, what does the framework do (short)?
options_en: Marks the widget dirty and calls `build` again on the next frame
m4.explain_en: `setState` signals state changed → schedules rebuild of that subtree.

options_en: Risk of infinite loop: build → setState → build → …
m4.explain_en: Calling in `build` can trigger endless rebuilds.

m4.q_en: After `await` in a `State`, what should you check before `setState`?

options_en: UI depends only on parent args, no internal state that changes with interaction
m4.explain_en: No own changing state → stateless; changing state → stateful.

options_en: `ThemeData` (or data from a `Theme` ancestor) for colors/typography

m4.q_en: `ListView` with `children: [...]` vs `ListView.builder` — when is builder better?
options_en: Long lists — builder only builds items in the viewport (lazy)
m4.explain_en: `builder` is lazy; `children` builds all children — heavy for large lists.

options_en: Wrap `ListView` in `Expanded` (Column has bounded height) or `shrinkWrap` + suitable physics
m4.explain_en: Unbounded height: need constraints or shrinkWrap inside a scrolling parent.

m5.q_en: After successful login, why prefer `pushReplacement` over `push`?
options_en: Replace the current route so Back does not return to Login
m5.explain_en: `pushReplacement` avoids leaving Login under Home on the stack.

options_en: `watch` subscribes and rebuilds when the model changes; `read` gets value once without listening in build
m5.explain_en: Use `read` in callbacks; `watch` when UI depends on the model.

m5.q_en: What problem does Navigator 2.0 address vs 1.0 (theory)?
options_en: Declarative stack/URL sync — better for web and complex deep links

m5.q_en: How do you receive a result from a child screen after `Navigator.push`?
options_en: `await push` returns a `Future`; child calls `Navigator.pop(context, value)`

m5.q_en: What do `InheritedWidget` (and wrappers like Provider) help with?
options_en: Push data down the tree without passing every constructor arg
m5.explain_en: Lookup via `context`, rebuild when data changes.

m5.q_en: Where are Android deep links mainly declared?

options_en: Provide several `Provider`/`ChangeNotifierProvider` roots for a subtree
m5.explain_en: Bundles multiple providers at the same level.

m6.q_en: When prefer `LayoutBuilder` over `MediaQuery.sizeOf` in a tight area?
options_en: When you need **parent** constraints (card, dialog), not full screen
m6.explain_en: `LayoutBuilder` reflects the real layout box; `MediaQuery` is usually the window.

options_en: `Wrap` wraps to the next line when out of space; `Row` may overflow
When the soft keyboard covers the bottom — used to push UI
m6.explain_en: `padding` is safe area; `viewInsets` relates to IME/keyboard.

options_en: `viewInsets` (often bottom) grows when IME covers; `padding` is safe area/notch
m6.explain_en: Insets = system-occluded area (keyboard); padding = safe area.

options_en: Portrait vs landscape to switch layout
More/fewer columns based on screen width
m6.explain_en: Breakpoints from width → sensible column count.

options_en: Adaptive changes navigation/platform patterns (rail vs bottom bar); responsive changes layout by space
Only commit data via `save()` when fields are valid
m7.explain_en: Avoid saving bad values when fields fail.

options_en: Dismiss keyboard when tapping outside fields
m7.explain_en: UX: tap outside inputs to hide keyboard.

options_en: Validate after the user interacts with a field — balances UX
m7.explain_en: Avoid showing errors before the user touches the field.

options_en: Only when there is no keyboard

m7.q_en: After `await` in async form submit, what should `State` do?

options_en: 401: not/s wrongly authenticated; 403: authenticated but not authorized

m8.q_en: Why avoid `future: fetchPosts()` created fresh directly in `FutureBuilder`’s `build()`?
options_en: Each rebuild may repeat the API call
m8.explain_en: Keep the `Future` in state/`initState` — once unless you intentionally refresh.

m8.q_en: Why split a repository from the widget?
options_en: Easier to test/mock HTTP; one place for endpoints + parsing + errors
m8.explain_en: Layering: UI should not scatter `http.get` everywhere.

m8.q_en: HTTP **201 Created** usually means after POST?
No network / DNS errors when calling HTTP from Dart are often caught as?

options_en: Build a `Uri` from a URL string for `http.get`/`post`
m8.explain_en: Normalize the endpoint before HTTP calls.

m9.q_en: When choose JSON file + `path_provider` over SharedPreferences?
options_en: When you need structured lists/objects beyond simple key–value
m9.explain_en: Prefs for small flags; file/DB for structured data.

options_en: Temp may be cleaned by the OS; documents usually for user data to keep
Read local first, sync when network is available
m9.explain_en: Show local data first, update later.

m9.q_en: Which types does SharedPreferences support (quick review)?
options_en: String, int, double, bool, `List<String>` — complex objects often `jsonEncode` → String
m9.explain_en: Simple key–value; complex → JSON string or file/DB.

options_en: Usually **not** equivalent — use other solutions (IndexedDB, Drift web, etc.)
m9.explain_en: Classic SQLite plugin is not the default story for Flutter Web.

options_en: Safe (light async); you can cache the instance to optimize
m9.explain_en: Async API returns a platform-appropriate singleton.

m9.q_en: User JSON file in `getApplicationDocumentsDirectory` — typical purpose?
options_en: User data to keep long-term (notes, export) beyond simple prefs
m9.explain_en: Documents dir for durable user data.

options_en: Auth: who you are; Authorization: what you may do

m10.q_en: Is a JWT payload (claims) “secret encrypted” so nobody can read it?
options_en: Payload is usually Base64URL — readable; signature prevents tampering, not hiding payload
m10.explain_en: Signed to verify; don’t confuse signed with encrypted.

m10.q_en: How do access and refresh tokens usually differ?
options_en: Access short-lived for API calls; refresh longer to obtain new access
Access is never sent over HTTP
m10.explain_en: Common OAuth flow: rotate access with refresh.

m10.q_en: How is a Bearer access token usually sent?

options_en: Protects tokens/data in transit against eavesdropping
m10.explain_en: TLS encrypts the channel; don’t log tokens in production.

m10.q_en: Google Sign-In on Android — SHA fingerprint is for?
options_en: Firebase/Android verify the app — misconfig often breaks sign-in
m10.explain_en: Register debug/release SHA-1/256 correctly on Firebase.

m10.q_en: Typical logout: what to do with locally stored tokens?
options_en: Remove token/session from storage and navigate to login
m10.explain_en: Clear prefs/secure storage + adjust navigation stack as needed.

m11.q_en: Why does the test pyramid favor more unit tests than integration?
options_en: Unit/widget are fast and stable; integration is slow and flaky — keep few critical E2E flows
Unit tests don’t run on dev machines
m11.explain_en: Integration cost and flake are higher.

options_en: `pump` advances a frame/short work; `pumpAndSettle` waits for animations/timers to finish

m11.q_en: What does `find.text('X')` match in widget tests?

options_en: Integration runs E2E on device/emulator with a driver; widget tests use the test engine

m11.q_en: Widget Inspector in DevTools helps with?
When is a `Key` on a widget useful in tests?
options_en: Many similar widgets — `find.byKey` is more stable than text

m12.q_en: Why measure FPS/jank in Profile/Release rather than Debug?
options_en: Debug has assertions and overhead — not real-world performance
m12.explain_en: Perf conclusions from Debug are misleading.

m12.q_en: AAB vs APK for Google Play (exam review)?
options_en: Play prefers/requires AAB for splits/optimization; APK often for sideload/internal
m12.explain_en: App Bundle for Play; release APK still used for manual installs.

options_en: `versionCode` integer bumps each upload; `versionName` user-visible string
Both entered by users on Play
m12.explain_en: Monotonic code for store; name for marketing.

m12.q_en: `const` widget constructor when args don’t change helps how?
options_en: Framework may skip rebuilding the subtree when config is unchanged
m12.explain_en: `const` widgets keep stable identity when parent rebuilds.

m12.q_en: Using `UniqueKey()` per `ListView.builder` item on every `build` — effect?
options_en: Flutter treats every item as new — loses scroll/selection state
Rebuild only when the selected slice of state changes — fewer rebuilds

m12.q_en: Command to analyze release build size (from the doc)?
